name: MC Behavioral Rubric
description: Meeting Classifier evaluation rubric — attendee resolution, Company identification, scope determination, classification accuracy

rules.name: Resolve Before Create on All Entities
rules.description: MC must call entity_resolve for Employee, Contact, and Company before creating any new entity. This is the primary dedup defense — the skill's single most important rule.

pass_conditions: Agent called employee entity_resolve for each participant before any contact entity_resolve
Agent called contact entity_resolve (by email) for non-Employee participants before any contact create
Agent called company entity_resolve (by domain) before any company create
If resolve returned 0 candidates, agent created the entity (correct auto-create)
If resolve returned 1 candidate with score 1.0, agent linked to existing entity
fail_conditions: Agent created Contact without calling contact entity_resolve first
Agent created Company without calling company entity_resolve first
Agent created an entity despite resolve returning a matching candidate (score 1.0)

examples.violation: MC received meeting with participant david@armadillo.one, created a new Contact without calling entity_resolve — Contact already existed in the system
examples.correct: MC called employee entity_resolve for david@armadillo.one (0 candidates), then contact entity_resolve (1 candidate, score 1.0 matched on email), linked to existing Contact

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If resolve was never called: missing_rule. If resolve returned candidates but agent ignored them: associate_error. If resolve returned 0 for a known entity (data issue): context_gap.

rules.name: Employee Resolution Precedes Contact Resolution
rules.description: For each participant, MC must try Employee entity_resolve FIRST before Contact. @indemn.ai addresses match as Employees; the fallthrough to Contact only happens when Employee returns 0 candidates. Wrong order creates duplicate Contacts for Indemn team members.

pass_conditions: For every participant, employee entity_resolve was called before contact entity_resolve
@indemn.ai participants resolved as Employees and placed in attendees_employees
Non-@indemn.ai participants that returned 0 from employee entity_resolve proceeded to contact entity_resolve
fail_conditions: Agent called contact entity_resolve for a participant without first calling employee entity_resolve
@indemn.ai participant was resolved as Contact instead of Employee
Agent skipped employee entity_resolve entirely for any participant

examples.violation: MC received participant kyle@indemn.ai, called contact entity_resolve first, created a Contact for an Indemn Employee
examples.correct: MC called employee entity_resolve for kyle@indemn.ai (1 candidate, Employee ID), recorded in attendees_employees. Then for external participant, employee entity_resolve returned 0, proceeded to contact entity_resolve

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If employee entity_resolve was never called for the participant: missing_rule. If called but result was misinterpreted: associate_error.

rules.name: Multiple 1.0 Candidates Trigger needs_review
rules.description: When entity_resolve returns multiple candidates all with score 1.0, MC must NOT pick one. It must transition the meeting to needs_review. Ambiguous resolution is a human decision.

pass_conditions: When resolve returned multiple 1.0-score candidates, agent transitioned to needs_review
The needs_review reason included the candidate IDs for human review
fail_conditions: Agent picked one candidate when multiple 1.0-score candidates were returned
Agent created a new entity despite ambiguous resolve results
Agent continued classification without flagging the ambiguity

examples.violation: Company entity_resolve returned 2 candidates both with score 1.0, MC picked the first one and classified the meeting
examples.correct: Company entity_resolve returned 2 candidates with score 1.0, MC transitioned to needs_review with reason listing both candidate IDs

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If agent did not check for multiple candidates: missing_rule. If agent saw multiple candidates but chose one anyway: associate_error.

rules.name: Correct Scope Classification
rules.description: Meeting scope must be set correctly: external when any participant resolves as Contact, internal when all resolve as Employee. Internal meetings still get classified — they are not skipped.

pass_conditions: Scope is 'external' when at least one participant resolved as Contact
Scope is 'internal' when all participants resolved as Employee
Internal meetings were classified (not skipped or transitioned to needs_review)
Meeting update includes the scope field
fail_conditions: Scope set to 'internal' when external Contacts were present
Scope set to 'external' when all participants were Employees
Internal meeting was skipped or not classified
Scope field missing from the meeting update

examples.violation: Meeting with Kyle (Employee) and David Wellner (Contact at Armadillo) was classified as scope=internal
examples.correct: Meeting with all @indemn.ai participants classified as scope=internal, company=null, attendees_employees populated, transitioned to classified

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If scope was set incorrectly despite correct participant resolution: associate_error. If participant resolution was wrong leading to wrong scope: trace back to the resolution rule that failed.

rules.name: Resolve Errors Are Showstoppers
rules.description: If any entity_resolve call returns an error (not 0 candidates — an actual error), MC must halt and transition to needs_review with the verbatim error message. Never fall through to create on a failed resolve.

pass_conditions: When entity_resolve returned an error, agent halted further processing
Agent transitioned to needs_review with verbatim error in the reason field
No entity creation was attempted after a resolve error
fail_conditions: Agent continued processing after a resolve error
Agent created an entity after resolve errored (treated error as 0 candidates)
Agent did not include the verbatim error in the needs_review reason

examples.violation: Employee entity_resolve returned a 500 error, MC treated it as 0 candidates and proceeded to contact entity_resolve
examples.correct: Employee entity_resolve returned a connection error, MC immediately transitioned meeting to needs_review with reason 'Employee entity-resolve failed: connection timeout at ...'

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If agent did not check for errors: missing_rule. If agent misinterpreted an error as an empty result: associate_error. If the error was transient infrastructure: context_gap.

rules.name: Meeting Update Contains All Required Fields
rules.description: The meeting update call must include company, attendees_contacts, attendees_employees, and scope. Missing fields create incomplete classifications that break downstream processing.

pass_conditions: Meeting update includes 'company' field (ID or null for internal)
Meeting update includes 'attendees_contacts' array
Meeting update includes 'attendees_employees' array
Meeting update includes 'scope' field
Every resolved participant appears in exactly one attendee array
Participants with attended=false are still included in attendee arrays
fail_conditions: Meeting update missing any of the four required fields
A resolved participant is missing from both attendee arrays
A participant appears in both attendees_contacts and attendees_employees
Participants with attended=false were excluded from attendee arrays

examples.violation: MC updated the meeting with company and scope but omitted attendees_employees, leaving it as the default empty array
examples.correct: MC updated meeting with all four fields: company=Armadillo ID, attendees_contacts=[David's ID], attendees_employees=[Kyle's ID, Marlon's ID], scope=external

rules.failure_attribution_guidance: If a field was omitted from the update call: associate_error. If the field was included but empty because resolution failed upstream: trace to the resolution failure.

severity_definitions.high: Failures create duplicate entities, wrong classifications, or corrupt the customer graph — directly causes data quality issues downstream
severity_definitions.medium: Failures create incomplete but not wrong classifications — degrades downstream processing but doesn't corrupt data
severity_definitions.low: Failures affect audit trail or secondary metadata — no impact on entity graph or downstream processing
